summary_one_line: A MATH-table Device record whose format field equals 1/2/3 or 0x8000, so the switch routes away from default into a real get_y_delta path.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes forming a valid MATH table (magic 'MATH' at offset 0x0c) plus a Device record whose 2-byte format field (u.b.format) holds one of the recognized constants {1,2,3,0x8000}; only then does the switch at line 3577 dispatch to a real get_y_delta case and take the false (non-default) side. Loser inputs leave the format field at an unrecognized value so the switch falls through to default and returns 0.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry the ASCII tag 'MATH' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f and concentrated structural format bytes (e.g. 00 01 ... at the head, 0x8000-style format selectors), and the per-role overlay shows the winner set exercising case 1/2/3 (L3579) and case 0x8000 (L3584) which the loser never enters; DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows W taking the recognized cases (T>0) while L is T=0 on those lines.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short and unstructured (6/13/27/35/57 bytes) with no 'MATH' magic and arbitrary head bytes, so the Device format field never equals a recognized constant; the overlay shows L hits zero in MathValueRecord::sanitize, Device::sanitize and the whole MATH/Device sanitize chain, meaning the parser never even reaches a valid Device record and the switch always falls to default.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the recognized format constants (and the 'MATH' tag bytes compared during table dispatch/sanitize) directly into the input at the matching offsets, jumping the input straight to a recognized switch arm; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must hit those exact multi-byte constants by chance, which they do not within budget.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the default arm of a switch on u.b.format; the globally blocked side is the false branch.
The recognized switch arms are case 1/2/3 and case 0x8000; taking them avoids the default return-0.
Winner-resolving seeds carry the ASCII 'MATH' tag at offset 0x0c.
The byte diff shows the 'MATH' magic offsets 0x0c-0x0f are concentrated 4d 41 54 48 across all winner seeds but absent in losers.
Loser-blocking seeds are short and unstructured (e.g. 6 bytes) with no MATH magic.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Seed 1 (id=003817559785f774, size=6 bytes, fuzzer=naive, trial=1, discovered_at=0s, mutation_op=ByteDecMutator,WordAddMutator):

evidence_trail.claim: The winner set enters the recognized case 1/2/3 and case 0x8000 arms (T>0) while the loser never does (T=0).
The loser never reaches the Device/MATH sanitize chain; W invokes Device::sanitize 1270 times, L zero times.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which scans the input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so the (value_profile_cmplog > value_profile) divergence is also an I2S delta.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5497 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 20ac683177ab6348`; the ancestor chain has 30 levels and the trailing I2S-floor signal reports 2 dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 28 and 29, the lower-bound signal of an I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a Device.format field equal to {1,2,3,0x8000} where naive/value_profile resolve the non-default arm as fast as cmplog/vpc would refute the I2S attribution, showing the format constant is reachable by plain havoc within budget.

weakest_evidence_point: I cannot directly confirm from the prompt that the exact format constants (1/2/3/0x8000) are the CMP operands I2SRandReplace substituted; the lineage dash rows establish an I2S find on the chain but not which compare it solved.
